step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("autophagy-related long non-coding RNAs in colorectal cancer prognosis")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

do you agree or disagree with Agent1’s original claim that "autophagy-related lncRNAs may have prognostic utility..."? Give exactly two sentences summarizing your revised position, then state your final answer choice.
After reviewing the retrieved evidence, there is no mention of autophagy-related long non-coding RNAs in any of the documents—only references to APC gene mutations, hereditary syndromes like FAP and Lynch syndrome, tumor-suppressor genes, and general cancer prognosis metrics unrelated to lncRNA functionality. Since no data link autophagy-related lncRNAs directly to colorectal cancer prognosis, the proposed connection lacks empirical support.
